rationale: The citing paper uses Van Heerde, Gijsbrechts, and Pauwels (2008) as the key prior empirical study on the Dutch supermarket price war and explicitly positions its own contribution against it. It summarizes that paper's core findings (e.g., increased price sensitivity, heterogeneous retailer outcomes) and uses them to motivate hypotheses about brand-level outcomes in price-war and post-price-war scenarios. The cited study also anchors important contextual elements of the natural experiment setting (the Dutch war timeline and characterization), not just a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper directly extends Van Heerde et al. (2008) by shifting the analysis from retailer-level winners and losers to supplier/brand-level performance before, during, and after the same Dutch price war. It builds on the cited paper's documented market effects to develop its own conceptual framing and empirical tests.
